description: Part 4 of Flashcards Set 2 (Policy provisions, group & health insurance basics)

questions.question: The federal income tax treatment of employer-provided group health insurance can be accurately described as
options: Employee's coverage paid for by the employer is considered taxable income to the employee
Employee's coverage paid for by the employer is tax-deductible to the employer as a business expenditure
Employer is given tax credits for contributions made to an employer-provided group health plan
Benefits are taxable to the employee
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Identify what the question is asking (rule, calculation, or outcome).
Step 2: Apply the LLQP rule or formula that governs the scenario.
Step 3: Eliminate distractors using the facts and remove options that conflict with the scenario.
Step 4: Select the best answer. Option B: Employee's coverage paid for by the employer is tax-deductible to the employer as a business expenditure.
optionFeedback: "Employee's coverage paid for by the employer is considered taxable income to the employee" is a different group plan feature than the one this question is testing. The better answer here is "Employee's coverage paid for by the employer is tax-deductible to the employer as a business expenditure".
Employee's coverage paid for by the employer is tax-deductible to the employer as a business expenditure.

"Employer is given tax credits for contributions made to an employer-provided group health plan" is a different group plan feature than the one this question is testing. The better answer here is "Employee's coverage paid for by the employer is tax-deductible to the employer as a business expenditure".

"Benefits are taxable to the employee" is a different group plan feature than the one this question is testing. The better answer here is "Employee's coverage paid for by the employer is tax-deductible to the employer as a business expenditure".

questions.question: Which of these statements accurately describes the Waiver of Premium provision in an Accident and Health policy?
options: Past due premiums on a lapsed policy are waived and coverage is restored
The insured is paid a monthly benefit to keep insurance premiums current in the event of total disability
Premiums are waived after the insured has been unemployed for a specified time period
Premiums are waived after the insured has been totally disabled for a specified time period
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Identify what the question is asking (rule, calculation, or outcome).
Step 2: Apply the LLQP rule or formula that governs the scenario.
Step 3: Eliminate distractors using the facts and remove options that conflict with the scenario.
Step 4: Select the best answer. Option D: Premiums are waived after the insured has been totally disabled for a specified time period.
optionFeedback: "Past due premiums on a lapsed policy are waived and coverage is restored" is a different policy provision than the one this question is testing. The correct answer is "Premiums are waived after the insured has been totally disabled for a specified time period" because this feature keeps the policy in force when the insured qualifies for a disability waiver.

"The insured is paid a monthly benefit to keep insurance premiums current in the event of tota..." is a different statement than the one this question is testing. The correct answer is "Premiums are waived after the insured has been totally disabled for a specified time period" because this feature keeps the policy in force when the insured qualifies for a disability waiver.

"Premiums are waived after the insured has been unemployed for a specified time period" is a different policy provision than the one this question is testing. The correct answer is "Premiums are waived after the insured has been totally disabled for a specified time period" because this feature keeps the policy in force when the insured qualifies for a disability waiver.
Premiums are waived after the insured has been totally disabled for a specified time period.
